rationale: The observation's base_record_id ('project_14th-street-transit-truck-priority-pilot') is already the closest-matching registry record. The payload aligns on project_name ('14th Street Transit & Truck Priority Pilot Project'), project_type ('pilot'), borough ('Manhattan'), and description. The slight status difference ('implemented October 2019' in the observation vs 'ongoing' in the registry) reflects a document-time snapshot, not a distinct identity. Per canonicalization rules, when the closest match IS the observation's own base_record_id, the decision is 'new' — the observation already resolves to that id.

The observation's base_record_id 'corridor_14th-street-ttp' already correctly matches the existing registry record for the 14th Street TTP Corridor. The source '14th_street_preliminary_report_fall_2019_corrected' is already listed in that record's source_ids. Corridor name (14th Street TTP Corridor), street (14th Street), borough (Manhattan), and limits (3rd Avenue to 8th/9th Avenue) all align. No linking to a different record is needed.
